prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(report_incident_at_site site1_primary_section)
(report_incident_at_site site1_secondary_section)
(report_incident_at_site plan_alpha)
(report_incident_at_site plan_bravo)
(assign_assessment_team_to_site site1_primary_section assessment_team_a)
(assign_assessment_team_to_site site1_secondary_section assessment_team_b)
(assign_sampling_kit_to_site site1_primary_section sampling_kit_1)
(finalize_assessment_for_site site1_primary_section sampling_kit_1)
(designate_local_staging_point site1_primary_section local_staging_point_1 sampling_kit_1)
(deploy_response_team_to_site site1_primary_section decon_team_1)
(validate_primary_section_with_response_team site1_primary_section local_staging_point_1 decon_team_1)
(release_response_team_from_site site1_primary_section decon_team_1)
(release_sampling_kit_from_site site1_primary_section sampling_kit_1)
(assign_sampling_kit_to_site site1_secondary_section sampling_kit_1)
(finalize_assessment_for_site site1_secondary_section sampling_kit_1)
(designate_secondary_staging_point_for_salvage site1_secondary_section secondary_staging_point_1 sampling_kit_1)
(deploy_response_team_to_site site1_secondary_section decon_team_1)
(validate_secondary_section_with_response_team site1_secondary_section secondary_staging_point_1 decon_team_1)
(release_response_team_from_site site1_secondary_section decon_team_1)
(release_sampling_kit_from_site site1_secondary_section sampling_kit_1)
(assemble_recovery_package site1_primary_section site1_secondary_section local_staging_point_1 secondary_staging_point_1 recovery_package_1)
(assign_sampling_kit_to_site site1_primary_section sampling_kit_1)
(verify_recovery_package_quality recovery_package_1 site1_primary_section sampling_kit_1)
(release_sampling_kit_from_site site1_primary_section sampling_kit_1)
(execute_salvage_on_primary_section site1_primary_section recovery_package_1)
(execute_salvage_on_secondary_section site1_secondary_section recovery_package_1)
(assign_sampling_kit_to_site site1_primary_section sampling_kit_1)
(assign_recovery_permit_to_site site1_primary_section recovery_permit_1 sampling_kit_1)
(complete_recovery_of_primary_section_and_release_resources site1_primary_section assessment_team_a recovery_permit_1)
(release_sampling_kit_from_site site1_primary_section sampling_kit_1)
(assign_sampling_kit_to_site site1_secondary_section sampling_kit_1)
(assign_recovery_permit_to_site site1_secondary_section recovery_permit_1 sampling_kit_1)
(complete_recovery_of_secondary_section_and_release_resources site1_secondary_section assessment_team_b recovery_permit_1)
(release_sampling_kit_from_site site1_secondary_section sampling_kit_1)
(assign_assessment_team_to_site plan_alpha assessment_team_a)
(assign_sampling_kit_to_site plan_alpha sampling_kit_1)
(finalize_assessment_for_site plan_alpha sampling_kit_1)
(validate_salvage_batch_and_assign_to_package plan_alpha salvage_batch_1 recovery_package_1)
(confirm_salvage_batch_for_plan plan_alpha salvage_batch_1 recovery_package_1 sampling_kit_1)
(bind_specialized_equipment_to_plan plan_alpha special_equipment_1)
(deploy_response_team_to_site plan_alpha decon_team_1)
(allocate_specialized_equipment_to_plan plan_alpha special_equipment_1 decon_team_1 salvage_batch_1 recovery_package_1)
(release_response_team_from_site plan_alpha decon_team_1)
(bind_external_expert_to_plan plan_alpha external_expert_1)
(complete_plan_validation_with_external_expert plan_alpha external_expert_1 salvage_batch_1 recovery_package_1)
(finalize_plan_for_execution_without_logistics plan_alpha)
(assign_recovery_permit_to_site plan_alpha recovery_permit_1 sampling_kit_1)
(complete_recovery_plan_and_release_resources plan_alpha assessment_team_a recovery_permit_1)
(release_sampling_kit_from_site plan_alpha sampling_kit_1)
(unbind_specialized_equipment_from_plan plan_alpha special_equipment_1)
(unbind_external_expert_from_plan plan_alpha external_expert_1)
(assign_assessment_team_to_site plan_bravo assessment_team_b)
(assign_sampling_kit_to_site plan_bravo sampling_kit_1)
(finalize_assessment_for_site plan_bravo sampling_kit_1)
(validate_salvage_batch_and_assign_to_package plan_bravo salvage_batch_2 recovery_package_1)
(confirm_salvage_batch_for_plan plan_bravo salvage_batch_2 recovery_package_1 sampling_kit_1)
(bind_specialized_equipment_to_plan plan_bravo special_equipment_1)
(deploy_response_team_to_site plan_bravo decon_team_1)
(allocate_specialized_equipment_to_plan plan_bravo special_equipment_1 decon_team_1 salvage_batch_2 recovery_package_1)
(release_response_team_from_site plan_bravo decon_team_1)
(bind_external_expert_to_plan plan_bravo external_expert_1)
(complete_plan_validation_with_external_expert plan_bravo external_expert_1 salvage_batch_2 recovery_package_1)
(finalize_plan_for_execution_without_logistics plan_bravo)
(assign_recovery_permit_to_site plan_bravo recovery_permit_1 sampling_kit_1)
(complete_recovery_plan_and_release_resources plan_bravo assessment_team_b recovery_permit_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves recovering two site sections: site1_primary_section and site1_secondary_section, and two plans: plan_alpha and plan_bravo. The goal is to have recovery_confirmed for both sections and both plans.


## Examining Recovery Goals  
- The task requires recovering site1_primary_section and site1_secondary_section, ensuring both are fully restored.  
- It also involves implementing recovery for plan_alpha and plan_bravo, with both plans needing confirmation.  
- The overall objective is to achieve recovery_confirmed status for all sections and plans.

## Examining Recovery Goals  
- Recovery_confirmed requires both site sections (site1_primary_section, site1_secondary_section) and plans (plan_alpha, plan_bravo) to be processed.  
- All elements—sections and plans—are classified as production_site, enabling shared actions like report_incident_at_site.  
- Specific actions like complete_recovery_of_primary_section_and_release_resources target sections, while complete_recovery_plan_and_release_resources handle plans.  

## Investigating Prerequisites  
- Before recovery, incidents must be reported for each production_site, including sections and plans, starting with report_incident_at_site.  
- Actions like ready_for_recovery require execute_salvage_on_... for sections or plan_marked_ready_for_execution for plans, with additional preconditions like permit_assigned.  
- Resource constraints, such as one sampling_kit_1 and one decon team, may require careful sequencing to handle shared dependencies.

## Investigating Prerequisites  
- Plan validation requires multiple steps, such as complete_plan_validation_with_external_expert, needing resources_bound, linked_external, and an associated batch.  
- Other paths like confirm_expert_binding_and_mark_logistics_ready or finalize_plan_for_execution_without_logistics set plans as ready_for_recovery, depending on resource availability.  

## Exploring Plan Validation Paths  
- Actions like attach_regulatory_certificate_to_plan require assessment_completed and sampling_assigned, setting the plan as attached if regulatory is available.  
- Finalize_plan_after_expert_review sets marked_ready and ready_for_recovery after record_external_expert_review confirms audit and expert input.  

## Analyzing Resource Constraints  
- Shared resources like one sampling_kit, decon_team, and recovery_permit require careful sequencing for sections and plans.  
- Sections share recovery_package_1, needing both prepared and validated before plans can use it, suggesting sections be processed first.  

## Mapping Recovery Process  
- Execute_salvage_on_primary_section requires prepared status, validated_salvage, and quality checks to set ready_for_recovery.  
- Complete_recovery_of_primary_section_and_release_resources confirms recovery, releasing team and permit, mirroring the process for plans.

## Sequencing Resource Assignments  
- Sampling_kit_1 and recovery_permit_1 are shared, requiring release and reassignment for multiple sites, like assigning to site1_primary_section then reusing.  
- Decon teams, with three available, must be managed, such as using team_c for a plan while waiting to free team_a or team_b from completed sections.  

## Planning Plan Execution  
- For plan_alpha, start with report_incident_at_site, then assign assessment_team_c and sampling_kit_1 for assessment completion.  
- Use assembled recovery_package_1 and validate_salvage_batch_and_assign_to_package to prepare for resource binding, ensuring no equipment is required.  

## Managing Equipment and Teams  
- Bind_specialized_equipment_to_plan, like special_equipment_1 to plan_alpha, requires assessment completion and sets equipment as linked, not available.  
- Deploy_response_team_to_site, such as decon_team_1 to plan_alpha, needs an available team and assessment completion, ensuring resources are bound properly.

## Ordering Incident Reports  
- Report incidents for site1_primary_section, site1_secondary_section, plan_alpha, and plan_bravo, as reporting has no cost and can be done upfront.  
- This step ensures all sites and plans are logged, setting the stage for further assessments.  

## Assigning Assessment Teams  
- Assign assessment_team_a to site1_primary_section and assessment_team_b to site1_secondary_section, ensuring teams are available and sites are not under assessment.  
- This leaves assessment_team_c available, with both primary and secondary sites now under assessment.  

## Managing Sampling Kit Assignments  
- Assign sampling_kit_1 to site1_primary_section for assessment, then finalize the assessment, completing it for the primary section.  
- Release sampling_kit_1 after finalizing, making it available for secondary section assessment, ensuring resource reuse.  

## Preparing Primary Section  
- Designate local_staging_point_1 for site1_primary_section using sampling_kit_1, preparing for response deployment.  
- Deploy decon_team_1 to site1_primary_section, validate with the response team, and release decon_team_1, marking the primary section prepared and validated.  

## Preparing Secondary Section  
- Assign sampling_kit_1 to site1_secondary_section, finalize assessment, and designate secondary_staging_point_1, mirroring the primary process.  
- Deploy and release decon_team_1 for validation, ensuring the secondary section is also prepared and validated.  

## Assembling Recovery Package  
- Assemble recovery_package_1 using site1_primary_section, site1_secondary_section, and their staging points, linking them without requiring additional flags.  
- Assign sampling_kit_1 again to site1_primary_section, verify recovery_package_1 quality, and release sampling_kit_1, completing quality checks.

## Sequencing Incident Actions  
- List actions in a logical order, starting with reporting incidents for site1_primary_section, site1_secondary_section, plan_alpha, and plan_bravo.  
- Ensure assessment teams and sampling kits are assigned and released systematically, such as assigning sampling_kit_1 to sites and finalizing assessments before validation.  

## Handling Recovery Operations  
- Execute salvage on site1_primary_section and site1_secondary_section using recovery_package_1, ensuring proper resource allocation.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
